title: quercus × blufftonensis
intro: Quercus × blufftonensis, also known as Bluffton oak, is a hybrid species of oak tree (Quercus) that is native to the United States. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus × blufftonensis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and address specific health issues.
